title: Reconnaissance Confirms Missile Installations on Allied Doorstep
description: Intelligence photographs confirm the rival superpower is installing nuclear-capable missiles within striking range of the mainland. The Joint Chiefs are recommending options ranging from a naval blockade to airstrikes. You have days, not weeks, to decide.
historicalContext: Inspired by the nuclear brinkmanship crises of the Cold War, when reconnaissance of missile installations forced a president into an extraordinarily narrow decision window.

choices.text: Order a naval blockade and demand withdrawal
choices.outcome: The fleet moves into position within hours. The world holds its breath. Congress is united behind you for the first time all year.

choices.text: Open back-channel negotiations before going public
choices.outcome: Quiet diplomacy buys time, but hawks in Congress accuse you of weakness before you can explain the strategy.

choices.text: Authorize precision airstrikes on the installations
choices.outcome: The strikes succeed militarily. Diplomats worldwide warn the response could spiral beyond anyone's control.

delayed_effects.headline: Allied capitals express alarm at the speed of the escalation

choices.text: Take the case to the United Nations before acting
choices.outcome: The UN session buys diplomatic cover but costs precious time. Critics call it a delay dressed up as principle.

title: The World Waits for the Next Move
description: Whatever path you chose, the standoff has not resolved itself. Both sides are signaling and posturing through back-channels while the public watches every broadcast for signs of how close the world really is to the brink.

choices.text: Accept a face-saving withdrawal agreement for both sides
choices.outcome: Both sides quietly stand down. History will remember it as the moment the world stepped back from the edge.

choices.text: Demand a public, unconditional capitulation
choices.outcome: The public celebrates a clear victory. Diplomats worry the humiliation will breed a more dangerous rival for years to come.

choices.text: Propose a formal arms limitation framework as part of the resolution
choices.outcome: The crisis becomes the unlikely foundation for the first real arms control talks in a generation.

choices.text: Let military advisors manage the drawdown quietly
choices.outcome: The standoff fades from headlines within weeks. No one is quite sure who backed down first, which suits everyone involved.

title: Advisors Report Rival Weapons Flowing Into a Regional Conflict
description: A regional civil conflict is quietly becoming a proxy battlefield. Intelligence confirms the rival superpower is arming one faction. Your own military advisors want to match the commitment before the balance tips.

choices.text: Send military advisors and equipment to the opposing faction
choices.outcome: The commitment is modest but real. Critics warn about how these commitments have a way of growing.

choices.text: Provide humanitarian aid but stay out of the arms flow
choices.outcome: Aid workers praise the approach. Hawks in Congress call it a missed opportunity to contain the rival's influence.

choices.text: Match the rival's commitment dollar for dollar and weapon for weapon
choices.outcome: The regional balance holds. The conflict grinds on longer and bloodier than anyone in the briefing room predicted.

choices.text: Push for an internationally brokered ceasefire instead
choices.outcome: The ceasefire push earns praise abroad. Both factions ignore it within weeks, and the fighting continues.

title: Space Agency Requests a Decision on the Next Launch Window
description: The space program stands at a genuine crossroads: a bold, risky mission that could cement national prestige, or a slower, safer path that preserves the budget for other priorities. The rival superpower's program is watching closely too.

choices.text: Commit to an ambitious, high-profile mission timeline
choices.outcome: The announcement dominates headlines for weeks. Engineers privately worry the timeline is aggressive to the point of risky.

choices.text: Fund a slower, more conservative program
choices.outcome: The program proceeds safely and quietly. It generates far fewer headlines than the rival's more dramatic milestones.

choices.text: Propose a joint scientific mission with the rival superpower
choices.outcome: The proposal is diplomatically bold. Hardliners on both sides call it naive; scientists call it overdue.

choices.text: Redirect space funding toward military satellite programs
choices.outcome: The public space program stalls while military applications quietly accelerate.

title: Senior Rival Diplomat Requests Asylum
description: A senior official from the rival superpower's embassy has walked into a field office requesting asylum — and claiming to carry documents detailing the other side's true military capabilities and intentions.

choices.text: Grant asylum immediately and publicize the defection
choices.outcome: The propaganda value is immense. The rival government issues furious denunciations, which only make it look worse for them.

choices.text: Grant asylum quietly and debrief without publicity
choices.outcome: Intelligence gains are substantial. Without the propaganda spotlight, the diplomatic fallout stays contained.

choices.text: Verify the intelligence claims before making any decision
choices.outcome: The caution pays off — some claims check out, others don't. The delay draws quiet criticism from the intelligence community.

choices.text: Decline asylum to avoid a diplomatic incident
choices.outcome: The decision avoids one incident and creates another — allies quietly question whether you can be trusted with a defector's safety.

title: Rival Government Signals Openness to Arms Talks
description: Through back channels, the rival superpower has signaled genuine interest in formal arms limitation talks — the first such opening in years. Your advisors are split on whether it's a real opportunity or a stalling tactic.

choices.text: Accept and schedule a full summit
choices.outcome: The summit is announced to global fanfare. Hardliners on both sides warn against expecting too much.

choices.text: Insist on preliminary verification measures first
choices.outcome: The cautious approach slows momentum but strengthens the eventual deal's credibility with skeptics.

choices.text: Decline, citing recent provocations
choices.outcome: Hawks at home approve. Allies abroad worry a genuine opening has been squandered.

choices.text: Propose expanding the talks to include allied nations
choices.outcome: Allies welcome the inclusion. The rival government calls it an attempt to gang up on them at the table.

title: Campus and City Demonstrations Draw National Attention
description: A wave of demonstrations against military spending and troop commitments has spread from campuses to major cities. Organizers are calling for a national day of action. The optics — and the politics — cut in every direction.

choices.text: Meet with movement leaders and acknowledge their concerns
choices.outcome: The meeting draws praise for openness and criticism from hardliners who call it a legitimizing gesture.

choices.text: Deploy additional security around planned demonstration sites
choices.outcome: The heavy security presence prevents disorder but generates its own wave of unflattering coverage.

choices.text: Give a national address defending the current defense posture
choices.outcome: The speech rallies the base. It does nothing to quiet the demonstrations, which continue regardless.

choices.text: Announce a review of troop commitment levels
choices.outcome: The announcement takes real wind out of the movement's sails. Military leadership privately expresses frustration.

title: Rival Bloc Requests Emergency Grain Purchases
description: A poor harvest has left the rival superpower facing real food shortages. Their government has quietly asked to purchase American grain reserves at scale — an economic opportunity wrapped in an awkward political question.

choices.text: Approve the sale — grain is grain, and farmers need the market
choices.outcome: Farm-state economies get a real boost. Hardliners accuse you of propping up an adversary's failing system.

choices.text: Approve the sale but attach human rights conditions
choices.outcome: The conditions draw praise from human rights advocates. The rival government calls it an insult wrapped in commerce.

choices.text: Deny the sale to maintain economic pressure
choices.outcome: Hardliners approve of the pressure. Farm-state legislators are furious about the lost market.

choices.text: Route the sale through international humanitarian channels
choices.outcome: The arrangement threads the needle diplomatically, though it takes longer to actually deliver the grain.

title: Congressional Committee Demands Loyalty Testimony From Officials
description: A congressional committee has launched aggressive hearings into alleged Communist sympathizers within the federal government, subpoenaing several of your own appointees. Civil libertarians call it a witch hunt; the committee chair calls it overdue vigilance.

choices.text: Publicly defend your appointees and criticize the hearings
choices.outcome: Civil libertarians praise the stand. The committee chair accuses you of being soft on the threat.

choices.text: Cooperate fully and let the process play out
choices.outcome: The hearings proceed with your cooperation. Several careers are damaged on thin evidence, and history will judge it harshly.

choices.text: Quietly reassign the officials under scrutiny
choices.outcome: The quiet reassignment defuses the immediate confrontation. It satisfies no one and looks evasive to everyone.

choices.text: Push for a Justice Department review of the committee's own conduct
choices.outcome: The countermove is bold and controversial. It permanently damages your relationship with the committee's allies in Congress.

title: Cities Debate Mandatory Fallout Shelter Requirements
description: Major cities are debating whether to mandate fallout shelters in new construction. Supporters call it basic preparedness; critics say it normalizes the idea of nuclear war and terrifies the public for no real protective benefit.

choices.text: Endorse a national civil defense construction standard
choices.outcome: The program moves forward. Critics say the shelters offer false comfort while normalizing catastrophe.

choices.text: Leave the decision entirely to local governments
choices.outcome: Cities go their own way. The patchwork result satisfies no one but avoids a national controversy.

choices.text: Redirect the funding toward diplomatic de-escalation efforts instead
choices.outcome: The reframe draws praise from peace advocates and sharp criticism from those who call it dangerously complacent.

choices.text: Launch a public information campaign on preparedness without new mandates
choices.outcome: The campaign is well-received as a middle path, though some call it preparedness theater.

title: Classified Defense Documents Found in a Reporter's Possession
description: A prominent investigative reporter has published excerpts from a classified defense assessment, raising urgent questions about a leak inside the administration — and about how much of the truth about the arms race has been kept from the public.

choices.text: Launch an aggressive internal investigation to find the source
choices.outcome: The investigation finds the leak but generates weeks of unflattering coverage about internal dysfunction.

choices.text: Pursue legal action against the publication
choices.outcome: The legal fight becomes a press-freedom flashpoint that dominates headlines far longer than the original leak.

choices.text: Declassify additional context to get ahead of the story
choices.outcome: The transparency earns credibility, though intelligence officials are furious about the precedent.

choices.text: Say nothing and let the story pass
choices.outcome: The silence doesn't work. Reporters fill the vacuum with speculation that's worse than the truth would have been.

title: Allied Nation's Government Teeters on the Brink of Collapse
description: A key allied government is facing a no-confidence vote amid economic turmoil and public anger over the cost of hosting American forces. If the government falls, the entire regional alliance structure could be thrown into question.

choices.text: Offer emergency economic assistance to stabilize the government
choices.outcome: The assistance package steadies the government, at least for now. Critics at home ask why American taxpayers are footing the bill.

choices.text: Publicly stay neutral on the domestic political matter
choices.outcome: The hands-off approach respects sovereignty but leaves allies uncertain about the depth of American commitment.

choices.text: Quietly signal support for opposition leaders more favorable to American interests
choices.outcome: The maneuver works in the short term. If it becomes public, it will look like exactly what it is.

choices.text: Reaffirm the alliance publicly without conditions
choices.outcome: The unconditional show of support calms markets and reassures the government, at least for the moment.

title: New Satellite Program Delivers Unprecedented Intelligence
description: A classified satellite reconnaissance program has achieved a breakthrough, providing detailed imagery of rival military installations previously hidden from view. The intelligence value is enormous — and so are the questions about how to use it.

choices.text: Use the intelligence to quietly recalibrate defense posture
choices.outcome: The recalibration is subtle enough that no one outside the program notices, exactly as intended.

choices.text: Share select findings with allied intelligence services
choices.outcome: Allies are grateful for the intelligence-sharing. Some in the program worry about the security risk of wider distribution.

choices.text: Keep the program's existence entirely secret, even from most of Congress
choices.outcome: Secrecy is maintained. When a handful of legislators eventually learn they were kept out of the loop, they are furious.

choices.text: Brief congressional leadership fully to secure long-term program funding
choices.outcome: The briefing secures durable bipartisan funding, at the cost of a wider circle knowing the program exists.

title: Pentagon Requests Expanded Conscription Authority
description: With troop commitments rising, the Pentagon is requesting expanded authority to draft additional personnel. The request lands in the middle of an already tense national debate over the fairness and necessity of the draft itself.

choices.text: Approve the expanded draft authority
choices.outcome: Military readiness improves. Draft boards across the country become the focal point of a new wave of protests.

choices.text: Approve a smaller expansion tied to volunteer incentive programs
choices.outcome: The compromise draws less fire than a straight expansion, though it doesn't fully solve the Pentagon's staffing needs.

choices.text: Deny the request and push for an all-volunteer force instead
choices.outcome: The decision is popular with the public and deeply unpopular with military planners worried about troop levels.

choices.text: Commission a review of draft fairness before deciding
choices.outcome: The review buys time and goodwill, but the Pentagon's staffing problem remains unresolved.

title: Markets Tumble on Fears of Imminent Confrontation
description: A wave of alarming rhetoric from both capitals has spooked markets, triggering the steepest single-week decline in years. Analysts are split on whether this is a genuine economic threat or a temporary panic that will pass.

choices.text: Issue a calming public statement emphasizing stability
choices.outcome: The statement helps steady nerves. Markets recover most of their losses within the week.

choices.text: Direct the Treasury to intervene directly in markets
choices.outcome: The intervention works, though critics question the precedent of the government propping up markets.

choices.text: Tone down public rhetoric to reduce tension
choices.outcome: The quieter tone helps markets and diplomacy alike, though hardliners accuse you of going soft under pressure.

choices.text: Let the market correct on its own without intervention
choices.outcome: The hands-off approach draws praise from free-market purists and criticism from everyone watching their savings shrink.

title: Secret Service Uncovers Credible Threat Against the President
description: Intelligence services have uncovered a credible, specific plot against your life, reportedly connected to extremist elements sympathetic to the rival superpower. Security recommendations range from a drastic lockdown of public appearances to a defiant show of normalcy.

choices.text: Accept a temporary lockdown of public appearances
choices.outcome: The lockdown likely saves lives. The public reads the absence as unsettling rather than reassuring.

choices.text: Continue the public schedule with enhanced security
choices.outcome: The defiant normalcy plays well publicly. The Secret Service is furious about the elevated risk.

choices.text: Use the plot as justification for expanded security legislation
choices.outcome: The legislation passes swiftly amid the scare. Civil liberties advocates warn about the precedent it sets.

choices.text: Keep the threat entirely confidential to avoid public panic
choices.outcome: The secrecy avoids panic. When details eventually surface, the lack of transparency becomes its own controversy.

title: Allies Push Back on Strict Technology Export Controls
description: Export controls meant to keep sensitive technology out of rival hands are frustrating allied governments and domestic manufacturers alike, who say the restrictions are costing them contracts to less-scrupulous competitors.

choices.text: Maintain strict controls despite the economic cost
choices.outcome: Security officials are pleased. Manufacturers and allied governments are increasingly frustrated.

choices.text: Loosen controls on clearly civilian technology
choices.outcome: Trade relationships improve immediately. Intelligence officials warn the line between civilian and military use is blurrier than it looks.

choices.text: Negotiate a coordinated allied export control framework
choices.outcome: The coordinated approach closes loopholes and eases some allied frustration, though negotiations take months.

choices.text: Create a fast-track review process for contested export cases
choices.outcome: The new process reduces friction without loosening the underlying policy — a rare genuine win-win.

title: Reconnaissance Aircraft Reported Down Near Contested Border
description: A reconnaissance aircraft has gone down near a heavily contested border, and it's unclear whether it was shot down or suffered mechanical failure. The rival government is demanding answers you're not yet sure how to give.

choices.text: Publicly acknowledge the mission and demand the crew's return
choices.outcome: The candor is politically costly abroad but earns respect at home for not hiding behind denials.

choices.text: Deny any knowledge of the mission publicly
choices.outcome: The denial collapses within days when contradicting evidence surfaces, damaging credibility badly.

choices.text: Open quiet diplomatic channels to negotiate the crew's release
choices.outcome: Quiet diplomacy makes real progress, out of the public eye where both sides can save face.

choices.text: Suspend the reconnaissance program pending review
choices.outcome: The suspension is a diplomatic olive branch. Intelligence officials warn it creates a dangerous blind spot.

title: Cultural Diplomacy Office Proposes an Artistic and Scientific Exchange
description: Career diplomats are pushing a modest but symbolically significant proposal: an exchange of musicians, scientists, and students with the rival bloc, aimed at building person-to-person understanding beneath the geopolitical freeze.

choices.text: Approve a full cultural and scientific exchange program
choices.outcome: The exchange becomes a modest but genuine bright spot in an otherwise tense relationship.

choices.text: Approve a limited student-only exchange
choices.outcome: The smaller program proceeds with little controversy and modest but real diplomatic value.

choices.text: Reject the proposal as premature given current tensions
choices.outcome: Hardliners approve. Career diplomats quietly call it a missed opportunity for genuine goodwill.

choices.text: Approve the exchange but require security vetting of all participants
choices.outcome: The compromise satisfies security concerns while preserving most of the program's diplomatic value.

title: Independent Analysts Claim a Dangerous 'Missile Gap'
description: A well-connected think tank has published an assessment claiming the rival superpower has quietly built a decisive lead in deployed missiles. The Pentagon's own numbers tell a murkier story, but the claim has already reached the evening broadcasts.
historicalContext: Inspired by the real 'missile gap' controversy, later shown to have significantly overstated the rival's actual missile deployment.

choices.text: Launch a crash program to close the alleged gap
choices.outcome: The buildup proceeds at speed. Years later, the gap the program was built to close turns out to have been mostly a paper tiger.

choices.text: Commission an independent classified assessment before acting
choices.outcome: The assessment takes months and finds the numbers murkier than either side's public claims. It quietly ends the panic.

choices.text: Publicly downplay the claim as politically motivated
choices.outcome: Critics accuse you of complacency in the face of a real threat. The accusation sticks longer than the denial does.

choices.text: Redirect the alarm toward conventional force readiness instead
choices.outcome: The pivot satisfies hawks without committing to the most expensive version of the buildup — a rare bit of budget discipline.

title: Intelligence Split on Whether a Star Defector Is Real
description: Months after a celebrated defection, a faction within the intelligence community now argues the defector was a deliberate plant, sent to feed false leads and burn real assets. Others insist the intelligence gathered so far has been too valuable to be disinformation.

choices.text: Continue acting on the intelligence as genuine
choices.outcome: Operations built on the intelligence proceed. If it's disinformation, the damage won't be visible for years.

choices.text: Quietly freeze all operations based on the intelligence pending review
choices.outcome: The freeze is the cautious call. Field officers grumble about real opportunities going stale while analysts argue.

choices.text: Publicly expose the defector as a suspected plant
choices.outcome: The public reversal is humiliating either way — either you exposed a real asset, or you handed the rival a propaganda win.

choices.text: Use the ambiguity itself as diplomatic leverage
choices.outcome: Letting the rival wonder what you actually know proves more useful than resolving the question ever would have.

title: Congress Moves to Rein In Unilateral Troop Deployments
description: A bipartisan group in Congress, alarmed by the growing pace of overseas troop commitments made without a vote, is pushing legislation requiring advance notification and time-limited authorization for future deployments.

choices.text: Accept the notification requirement as a reasonable check
choices.outcome: The gesture earns real goodwill in Congress, at some cost to how quickly you can move in a fast-moving crisis.

choices.text: Threaten a veto and defend executive deployment authority
choices.outcome: The veto threat holds. Congress backs down for now, but the relationship damage doesn't heal quickly.

choices.text: Negotiate a narrower compromise notification window
choices.outcome: The compromise satisfies neither side's purists but actually becomes workable law — a rarer outcome than it should be.

choices.text: Publicly assert an even broader reading of existing executive authority
choices.outcome: The assertion is bold and legally aggressive. Congressional leadership calls it a fight they didn't ask for and now intend to win.

title: Budget Hawks and Hardliners Collide Over Defense Spending
description: The annual defense appropriations fight has turned unusually bitter: fiscal conservatives want the topline capped, defense hardliners in the same party call any cap reckless given the rival's buildup, and neither faction is budging.

choices.text: Side with the budget hawks and hold the line on spending
choices.outcome: The cap holds. Hardliners in your own party are furious, and the Pentagon quietly revises its readiness projections downward.

choices.text: Side with the hardliners and approve the full request
choices.outcome: The Pentagon gets what it asked for. Fiscal hawks warn, not for the first time, that the bill always comes due eventually.

choices.text: Broker a middle-ground topline both factions can live with
choices.outcome: Nobody loves the number, which is usually the sign of a workable compromise in this town.

choices.text: Let the factions fight it out and stay above the fray publicly
choices.outcome: The standoff drags into a continuing resolution. Both factions privately blame you for not stepping in sooner.

title: Senior Official Revealed as a Long-Term Double Agent
description: A years-long counterintelligence investigation has confirmed the worst: a senior official with access to sensitive defense planning has been passing information to the rival superpower for the better part of a decade.

choices.text: Announce the arrest and order a full public accounting
choices.outcome: The transparency is politically costly but restores some public confidence that the problem is being taken seriously.

choices.text: Handle it quietly through classified channels
choices.outcome: The quiet handling limits the immediate damage. When a handful of legislators eventually learn the scope, they're furious at being kept out.

choices.text: Publicly attribute the failure to the previous administration's vetting
choices.outcome: The deflection plays well with the base. It also draws sharp, credible pushback from officials who know the timeline doesn't fully support it.

choices.text: Use the scandal to push through expanded counterintelligence funding
choices.outcome: The funding sails through amid the scare. Civil liberties advocates warn the expanded authority will outlive the crisis that justified it.

title: School Boards Nationwide Debate Banning 'Subversive' Books
description: A wave of local school board votes to remove books deemed sympathetic to the rival ideology has sparked a national free-speech controversy. Parents' groups are split, and the fight is spreading from libraries to state legislatures.

choices.text: Speak out publicly in defense of free expression
choices.outcome: Civil libertarians praise the stand. Parents' groups pushing the bans accuse you of siding against local communities.

choices.text: Stay out of what is fundamentally a local school board matter
choices.outcome: The neutrality avoids a national fight, though it satisfies no one directly involved in the local ones.

choices.text: Voice support for the removals, citing the security climate
choices.outcome: The base approves. Historians and civil libertarians alike warn this is exactly the kind of moment they'll be asked about later.

choices.text: Propose voluntary national curriculum guidelines to defuse the fight
choices.outcome: The guidelines are voluntary and toothless by design, which is exactly what lets both sides claim a partial win.

title: Advocates Push for Amnesty on Draft Evasion Charges
description: With troop commitments cooling in one theater, advocacy groups are pushing for amnesty for those who evaded the draft or deserted rather than serve. Veterans' organizations are deeply divided on whether that would honor or insult their own service.

choices.text: Grant a broad, unconditional amnesty
choices.outcome: Families who spent years afraid of prosecution finally exhale. Veterans' groups split loudly and publicly over the decision.

choices.text: Offer conditional amnesty tied to a period of public service
choices.outcome: The compromise draws less fury than a blanket pardon, and even some veterans' groups call it a fair middle path.

choices.text: Reject amnesty and maintain existing prosecutions
choices.outcome: Veterans' groups who opposed amnesty are relieved. Families still living under the threat of prosecution are not.

choices.text: Convene a veterans' advisory panel before deciding anything
choices.outcome: The panel buys time and political cover, though it leaves thousands of cases in limbo while it deliberates.

title: Advisors Propose a Direct Emergency Line Between Capitals
description: After a string of close calls where slow, garbled communication nearly turned a misunderstanding into a crisis, your national security team is pushing a direct, dedicated emergency communication line between the two capitals.
historicalContext: Inspired by the real establishment of a direct emergency communications link between rival Cold War capitals following a crisis that revealed dangerously slow diplomatic communication.

choices.text: Formally propose the hotline and push hard for it
choices.outcome: The proposal is well received. Even skeptics on both sides concede that faster communication reduces the odds of an accidental war.

choices.text: Signal openness but wait for the rival to propose it first
choices.outcome: The rival eventually raises it independently. The delay costs little, though your own team wonders why you didn't move first.

choices.text: Reject the idea as needless proximity to an adversary
choices.outcome: Hardliners approve of the caution. Your own national security advisors privately call it a missed chance to reduce real risk.

choices.text: Propose a multilateral version that includes key allies
choices.outcome: Allies appreciate the inclusion. The rival government calls the added parties an unnecessary complication and slows negotiations.

title: Naval Incident in Contested Waters Raises Alarm
description: A rival submarine and an allied naval vessel came dangerously close to collision in contested waters overnight. Both sides are trading accusations, and naval commanders on the scene are requesting guidance before the next patrol.

choices.text: Demand a formal apology and compensation
choices.outcome: The demand plays well domestically. The rival government calls it an overreaction to a routine navigational hazard.

choices.text: Open quiet naval-to-naval channels to establish incident protocols
choices.outcome: The quiet talks produce a real, lasting protocol for avoiding the next near-miss — unglamorous, genuinely useful diplomacy.

choices.text: Increase naval posture in the area in response
choices.outcome: The show of force is unmistakable. Naval planners on both sides now consider those waters measurably more dangerous.

choices.text: Downplay the incident publicly to avoid escalation
choices.outcome: The low-key response avoids a spiral. It also draws quiet criticism from commanders who felt the risk deserved a real response.

title: Scientists and Engineers Are Leaving for Better Offers Abroad
description: A quiet but real exodus of scientists and engineers — some to the private sector, a smaller but politically alarming number to the rival bloc itself — has advisors warning that pay and morale in government research have fallen dangerously behind.

choices.text: Raise pay and benefits for government scientists and engineers
choices.outcome: Retention improves within a year. Budget hawks note that competing with the private sector on salary is an expensive habit to start.

choices.text: Launch a patriotic recruitment and retention campaign
choices.outcome: The campaign helps at the margins. It doesn't move the underlying pay gap that's actually driving people to leave.

choices.text: Tighten travel and export restrictions on defense-linked scientists
choices.outcome: The restrictions slow the most alarming departures. Scientists broadly resent being treated as flight risks rather than professionals.

choices.text: Treat it as a private-sector personnel issue, not a government one
choices.outcome: Nothing changes. The exodus continues at the same quiet pace, and next year's briefing will note it again.
